label: BREAKING
hook: PUTIN CALLS UP 250,000 RESERVISTS AS DONBAS OFFENSIVE STALLS
stat1: Largest mobilization wave since February 2022 invasion
stat2: Russia's military-age male population shrinking fast
stat3: Russian stocks hit fresh lows on London exchange
tts_text: Breaking. Putin orders a partial mobilization of two hundred fifty thousand reservists as Ukraine's offensive stalls in eastern Donbas. The largest call-up since the invasion began.
caption:
Putin just ordered 250,000 reservists mobilized — the largest single conscription wave since the February 2022 invasion kicked off.

Here's what this really signals:

Russia isn't winning on the battlefield. Ukraine's counteroffensive in Donbas has momentum problems, but Moscow wouldn't be dredging the reserve pool if front-line attrition weren't severe. The Kremlin is buying time with manpower, not strategy. This also tells you Russia's professional volunteer force is depleted — they're moving to conscription because they have to.

The second-order effect: Russia's military-age male population is contracting. Fresh call-ups reduce the civilian labor force. Moscow has to choose between guns and economic output. You can't scale both indefinitely. London-listed Russian equities are pricing in that constraint — hitting fresh lows as the mobilization news landed.

This is not a sign of strength. It's a symptom of attrition Russia can't replace fast enough.

— The Clam Letter
